message:
feat(fp-io): write_sql identifier-length validation (br-9ynk / fd90.27)

Apply fd90.26's max_identifier_length() capability inside
write_sql_with_options. When the backend reports Some(n), validate
table name + every column name + index_label + schema name against
n bytes. Returns IoError::Sql identifying which identifier exceeded
the cap. SQLite (None) is unaffected — long identifiers remain
accepted on SQLite as before.

Helper validate_sql_identifier_length(name, max, kind) localizes
the check so the call site stays readable.

6 new tests cover:
- SQLite accepts 80-char column (None cap, no validation)
- PG-like 63-cap rejects 64-char column with clear error
- PG-like rejects 64-char table name
- PG-like rejects 64-char index label (when index=true)
- PG-like rejects 64-char schema name
- Boundary: 63-char identifier exactly fits and is accepted

329 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean across fp-io +
umbrella; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
